revision.comment: 片岡神社（住吉神社：吉田町）の記事を新規作成
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|thumb|center|]] |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]住吉2212-1 |位置 = |祭神 = [[底筒男命]]
[[中筒男命]]
[[表筒男命]]
[[息長帯毘売命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月2日、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[底筒男命]] * [[中筒男命]] * [[表筒男命]] * [[息長帯毘売命]] == 概要 == 社伝によると、[[大和国]]の片岡神社から勧請された古社で、[[式内社]]。 [[仁徳天皇]]の御代に[[摂津国]]の[[住吉大社]]から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになった{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/toutoumi/kataoka_title.htm |title = 片岡神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}が、[[明治維新]]後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 創建は不詳。[[正保]]4年（1647年）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 [[大正]]2年（1913年）、[[帝室技芸員]]の[[伊藤平左衛門]]の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:あくなみしんしや}} [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|thumb|center|]] |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]住吉2212-1 |位置 = |祭神 = [[底筒男命]]
[[中筒男命]]
[[表筒男命]]
[[息長帯毘売命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月2日、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[底筒男命]] * [[中筒男命]] * [[表筒男命]] * [[息長帯毘売命]] == 概要 == 社伝によると、[[大和国]]の片岡神社から勧請された古社で、[[式内社]]。 [[仁徳天皇]]の御代に[[摂津国]]の[[住吉大社]]から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになった{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/toutoumi/kataoka_title.htm |title = 片岡神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}が、[[明治維新]]後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 創建は不詳。[[正保]]4年（1647年）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 [[大正]]2年（1913年）、[[帝室技芸員]]の[[伊藤平左衛門]]の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かたおかしんしや}} [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

[[近代社格制度#諸社|旧郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月2日]]、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[住吉三神|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命]] * [[神功皇后|息長帯毘売命]] == 概要 == 社伝によると、[[大和国]]の[[片岡神社 (王寺町)|片岡神社]]から勧請された古社で、[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。 [[仁徳天皇]]の御代に[[摂津国]]の[[住吉大社]]から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになった{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/toutoumi/kataoka_title.htm |title = 片岡神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}が、[[明治維新]]後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 創建は不詳。[[正保]]4年（[[1647年]]）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、[[帝室技芸員]]の[[伊藤平左衛門]]の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かたおかしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]]

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

表筒男命]]
[[神功皇后|息長帯毘売命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
[[近代社格制度#諸社|旧郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月2日]]、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[住吉三神|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命]] * [[神功皇后|息長帯毘売命]] == 概要 == 社伝によると、[[大和国]]の[[片岡神社 (王寺町)|片岡神社]]から勧請された古社で、[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。 [[仁徳天皇]]の御代に[[摂津国]]の[[住吉大社]]から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになった{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/toutoumi/kataoka_title.htm |title = 片岡神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}が、[[明治維新]]後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 創建は不詳。[[正保]]4年（[[1647年]]）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、[[帝室技芸員]]の[[伊藤平左衛門]]の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かたおかしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]]

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|片岡神社 (吉田町)|version=106988949|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]住吉2212-1 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 32.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 18.5 |祭神 = [[住吉三神|底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命]]
[[神功皇后|息長帯毘売命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
[[近代社格制度#諸社|旧郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月2日]]、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[住吉三神|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命]] * [[神功皇后|息長帯毘売命]] == 概要 == 社伝によると、[[大和国]]の[[片岡神社 (王寺町)|片岡神社]]から勧請された古社で、[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。 [[仁徳天皇]]の御代に[[摂津国]]の[[住吉大社]]から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになった{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/toutoumi/kataoka_title.htm |title = 片岡神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}が、[[明治維新]]後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 創建は不詳。[[正保]]4年（[[1647年]]）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、[[帝室技芸員]]の[[伊藤平左衛門]]の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] ==End Japanese==

link|Q28682738}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|片岡神社 (吉田町)|version=106988949|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]住吉2212-1 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 32.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 18.5 |祭神 = [[住吉三神|底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命]]
[[神功皇后|息長帯毘売命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
[[近代社格制度#諸社|旧郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月2日]]、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[住吉三神|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命]] * [[神功皇后|息長帯毘売命]] == 概要 == 社伝によると、[[大和国]]の[[片岡神社 (王寺町)|片岡神社]]から勧請された古社で、[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。 [[仁徳天皇]]の御代に[[摂津国]]の[[住吉大社]]から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになった{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/toutoumi/kataoka_title.htm |title = 片岡神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}が、[[明治維新]]後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 創建は不詳。[[正保]]4年（[[1647年]]）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、[[帝室技芸員]]の[[伊藤平左衛門]]の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] ==End Japanese==

[[近代社格制度#諸社|旧郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月2日]]、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[榛原郡]][[吉田町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[住吉三神|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命]] * [[神功皇后|息長帯毘売命]] == 概要 == 社伝によると、[[大和国]]の[[片岡神社 (王寺町)|片岡神社]]から勧請された古社で、[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。 [[仁徳天皇]]の御代に[[摂津国]]の[[住吉大社]]から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになった{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/toutoumi/kataoka_title.htm |title = 片岡神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}が、[[明治維新]]後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 創建は不詳。[[正保]]4年（[[1647年]]）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、[[帝室技芸員]]の[[伊藤平左衛門]]の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]]

{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|旧郷社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|8月2日|Q2785}}、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|郷社}}。 == 祭神 == * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} == 概要 == 社伝によると、{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の{{jalink|片岡神社 (王寺町)|Q43594918|片岡神社}}から勧請された古社で、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。 {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の御代に{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}の{{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになった{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/toutoumi/kataoka_title.htm |title = 片岡神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}が、{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 == 歴史 == 創建は不詳。{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}4年（{{jalink|1647年|Q6935}}）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 {{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}2年）、{{jalink|帝室技芸員|Q11481067}}の{{jalink|伊藤平左衛門|Q16766564}}の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]]

{{nihongo|'''Kataoka Shrine'''|片岡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}住吉2212-1 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 32.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 18.5 |祭神 = {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命}}
{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|旧郷社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|8月2日|Q2785}}、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|郷社}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} === 概要 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の{{jalink|片岡神社 (王寺町)|Q43594918|片岡神社}}から勧請された古社で、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。 {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の御代に{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}の{{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになったが、{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 === 歴史 === 創建は不詳。{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}4年（{{jalink|1647年|Q6935}}）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 {{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}2年）、{{jalink|帝室技芸員|Q11481067}}の{{jalink|伊藤平左衛門|Q16766564}}の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 === 摂末社 === 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かたおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}住吉2212-1 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 32.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 18.5 |祭神 = {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命}}
{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|旧郷社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|8月2日|Q2785}}、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|郷社}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} === 概要 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の{{jalink|片岡神社 (王寺町)|Q43594918|片岡神社}}から勧請された古社で、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。 {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の御代に{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}の{{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになったが、{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 === 歴史 === 創建は不詳。{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}4年（{{jalink|1647年|Q6935}}）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 {{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}2年）、{{jalink|帝室技芸員|Q11481067}}の{{jalink|伊藤平左衛門|Q16766564}}の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 === 摂末社 === 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かたおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}住吉2212-1 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 32.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 18.5 |祭神 = {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命}}
{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|旧郷社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|8月2日|Q2785}}、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|郷社}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} === 概要 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の{{jalink|片岡神社 (王寺町)|Q43594918|片岡神社}}から勧請された古社で、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。 {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の御代に{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}の{{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになったが、{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 === 歴史 === 創建は不詳。{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}4年（{{jalink|1647年|Q6935}}）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 {{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}2年）、{{jalink|帝室技芸員|Q11481067}}の{{jalink|伊藤平左衛門|Q16766564}}の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 === 摂末社 === 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かたおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:pages]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}住吉2212-1 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 32.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 18.5 |祭神 = {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命}}
{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|旧郷社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|8月2日|Q2785}}、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|郷社}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} === 概要 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の{{jalink|片岡神社 (王寺町)|Q43594918|片岡神社}}から勧請された古社で、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。 {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の御代に{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}の{{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになったが、{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 === 歴史 === 創建は不詳。{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}4年（{{jalink|1647年|Q6935}}）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 {{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}2年）、{{jalink|帝室技芸員|Q11481067}}の{{jalink|伊藤平左衛門|Q16766564}}の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 === 摂末社 === 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かたおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}住吉2212-1 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 32.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 18.5 |祭神 = {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命}}
{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|旧郷社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|8月2日|Q2785}}、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|郷社}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} === 概要 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の{{jalink|片岡神社 (王寺町)|Q43594918|片岡神社}}から勧請された古社で、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。 {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の御代に{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}の{{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになったが、{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 === 歴史 === 創建は不詳。{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}4年（{{jalink|1647年|Q6935}}）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 {{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}2年）、{{jalink|帝室技芸員|Q11481067}}の{{jalink|伊藤平左衛門|Q16766564}}の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 === 摂末社 === 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かたおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = 片岡神社 |画像= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}住吉2212-1 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 32.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 18.5 |祭神 = {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命}}
{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|旧郷社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 住吉神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|8月2日|Q2785}}、10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''片岡神社'''（かたおかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|榛原郡|Q1203336}}{{jalink|吉田町|Q1202789}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|郷社}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長帯毘売命}} === 概要 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の{{jalink|片岡神社 (王寺町)|Q43594918|片岡神社}}から勧請された古社で、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}。 {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の御代に{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}の{{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}から分霊を勧請し、以後、住吉大明神（住吉神社）と称されるようになったが、{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後に片岡神社を正式社名とした。 境内に船体「住吉丸」が置かれている。 === 歴史 === 創建は不詳。{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}4年（{{jalink|1647年|Q6935}}）に社殿を建て替えた記録が残っている。 {{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}2年）、{{jalink|帝室技芸員|Q11481067}}の{{jalink|伊藤平左衛門|Q16766564}}の設計により、社殿を萱葺きから瓦葺に改築した。 戦後、幣殿を造営した{{Cite web|和書 |author = 片岡神社 |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = 片岡神社（由緒・社伝） |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 === 摂末社 === 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * 船玉神社 * 星宮神社 * 津島神社・稲荷神社・厳島神社 * 十二神神社・西の宮神社・琴平神社 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かたおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2026-01}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kataoka Shrine |Image= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Haibara District|Q1203336}}{{jalink|Yoshida Town|Q1202789}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|Former village shrine}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{jalink|August 2|Q2785}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Yoshida Town|Q1202789}}, {{jalink|Haibara District|Q1203336}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|village shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}’s {{jalink|Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|Kataoka Shrine}}, and it is listed as a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}. During the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} in {{jalink|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{jalink|Shōhō|Q1146192}} ({{jalink|1647|Q6935}}). In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (the 2nd year of {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{jalink|Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564}}, an {{jalink|Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kataoka Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2026-01}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kataoka Shrine |Image= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Haibara District|Q1203336}}{{jalink|Yoshida Town|Q1202789}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|Former village shrine}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{jalink|August 2|Q2785}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Yoshida Town|Q1202789}}, {{jalink|Haibara District|Q1203336}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|village shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}’s {{jalink|Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|Kataoka Shrine}}, and it is listed as a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}. During the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} in {{jalink|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{jalink|Shōhō|Q1146192}} ({{jalink|1647|Q6935}}). In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (the 2nd year of {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{jalink|Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564}}, an {{jalink|Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2026-01}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kataoka Shrine |Image= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Haibara District|Q1203336}}{{jalink|Yoshida Town|Q1202789}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|Former village shrine}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{jalink|August 2|Q2785}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Yoshida Town|Q1202789}}, {{jalink|Haibara District|Q1203336}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|village shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}’s {{jalink|Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|Kataoka Shrine}}, and it is listed as a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}. During the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} in {{jalink|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{jalink|Shōhō|Q1146192}} ({{jalink|1647|Q6935}}). In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (the 2nd year of {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{jalink|Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564}}, an {{jalink|Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kataoka Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:pages]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2026-01}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kataoka Shrine |Image= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Haibara District|Q1203336}}{{jalink|Yoshida Town|Q1202789}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|Former village shrine}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{jalink|August 2|Q2785}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Yoshida Town|Q1202789}}, {{jalink|Haibara District|Q1203336}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|village shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}’s {{jalink|Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|Kataoka Shrine}}, and it is listed as a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}. During the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} in {{jalink|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{jalink|Shōhō|Q1146192}} ({{jalink|1647|Q6935}}). In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (the 2nd year of {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{jalink|Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564}}, an {{jalink|Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kataoka Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kataoka Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]
