[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qq old]]

[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:吉田町の建築物]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Qq old]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2026-01}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kataoka Shrine |Image= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yoshida]] [[Category:吉田町の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Qq old]]

[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yoshida]] [[Category:History of Yoshida]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Qq old]]

[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yoshida]] [[Category:History of Yoshida]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Qq old]]

[[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|WD=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|WD=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|WD=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|WD=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|WD=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|WD=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yoshida]] [[Category:History of Yoshida]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]]

'静岡県榛原郡吉田町住吉2212-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:片岡神社 (吉田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q28682738}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2026-01}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kataoka Shrine |Image= [[File:Kataoka-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|qid=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|qid=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}} Sumiyoshi 2212-1 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 45 | Latitude seconds = 32.9 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 | Longitude seconds = 18.5 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|qid=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|qid=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|qid=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|qid=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|qid=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|qid=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|qid=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|qid=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|qid=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kataoka Shrine (Yoshida)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yoshida]] [[Category:History of Yoshida]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]]

Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Former village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Sumiyoshi Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=August 2|Q2785|qid=Q2785|1=August 2}}, second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Kataoka Shrine''' (かたおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida Town|Q1202789|qid=Q1202789|1=Yoshida, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Haibara District|Q1203336|qid=Q1203336|1=Haibara District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Village shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it is an ancient shrine that was invited from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji, Nara)|Q43594918|qid=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}}, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, after which it came to be known as Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin (Sumiyoshi Shrine). Following the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Kataoka Shrine was adopted as the official shrine name. Within the shrine grounds, the boat-shaped object “Sumiyoshi-maru” is displayed. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. Records indicate that the shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|qid=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|qid=Q6935|1=1647}}). In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|qid=Q2080|1=1913}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}), the shrine buildings were renovated from thatched roofing to tiled roofing, based on designs by {{ill|lt=Heisaemon Itō|Q16766564|qid=Q16766564|1=Heisaemon Itō}}, an {{ill|lt=Imperial Household Artist|Q11481067|qid=Q11481067|1=Imperial Household Artist}}. After the war, the heiden (offering hall) was constructed{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kataoka Shrine |url = http://homepage2.nifty.com/sugao/ |title = Kataoka Shrine (History and Shrine Tradition) |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === Auxiliary shrines === The auxiliary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Shrines within the grounds''' * Funatama Shrine * Hoshinomiya Shrine * Tsushima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine * Jūnishin Shrine, Nishinomiya Shrine, Kotohira Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
